qa_pairs.question: How are software packages installed within the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Software packages within the Alliance software environment are always installed using scripts.

qa_pairs.question: What are the primary tools used for installing software packages in the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: The primary tools used for installing software packages in the Alliance software environment include EasyBuild, Gentoo Overlays (starting in 2020), and formerly NixOS.

qa_pairs.question: Can the Alliance software environment be accessed from any location globally?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the Alliance software environment can be used on any computer in the world.

qa_pairs.question: What technology enables the Alliance software environment to be accessible globally?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance software environment is accessible on any computer in the world thanks to CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: How are changes and new installations to the Alliance software environment tracked?
qa_pairs.answer: All changes and new installations made to the Alliance software environment are tracked through Git.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find a list of recent changes to the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can see a list of recent changes to the Alliance software environment through various Git links provided for different components and CPU architectures.

qa_pairs.question: Which Git repository tracks changes to core modules and modules installed for the AVX2 CPU architecture within the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: The `https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-easyconfigs-installed-avx2/commits/main` repository tracks changes to the core modules and modules installed for the AVX2 CPU architecture.

qa_pairs.question: Where can one view changes related to the AVX512 CPU architecture modules in the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes related to the modules installed for the AVX512 CPU architecture in the Alliance software environment can be viewed at `https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-easyconfigs-installed-avx512/commits/main`.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of changes are tracked in the `software-stack-config` Git repository for the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: The `software-stack-config` Git repository tracks changes to the primary configuration files for the Alliance software environment.

qa_pairs.question: Which Git repository shows changes made to the EasyBuild configuration within the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes to the EasyBuild configuration within the Alliance software environment can be found in the `https://github.com/ComputeCanada/easybuild-computecanada-config/commits/main` repository.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find updates to custom modules and scripts in the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Updates to custom modules and scripts in the Alliance software environment are tracked at `https://github.com/ComputeCanada/software-stack-custom/commits/main`.

qa_pairs.question: Which module versions use the Gentoo layer for the Alliance software environment, and where are its changes tracked?
qa_pairs.answer: The Gentoo layer is used with `StdEnv/2020` and `StdEnv/2023` for the module `gentoo/YYYY`, and its core changes are tracked at `https://github.com/ComputeCanada/gentoo-overlay/commits/main`.

qa_pairs.question: Is NixOS still actively used for software installation in the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: No, NixOS was formerly used for software installation in the Alliance software environment, but it is now deprecated.

qa_pairs.question: Which module versions were associated with the deprecated Nix layer in the Alliance software environment?
qa_pairs.answer: The deprecated Nix layer, specifically `nixpkgs/16.09`, was used with `StdEnv/2016.4` and `StdEnv/2018.3` in the Alliance software environment.
